claim.observation: fls of the fredr user profile (inode 154910) lists, among standard profile folders, multiple distinct cloud-storage sync directories: 'Google Drive', 'iCloudDrive', 'iCloudPhotos', 'OneDrive', 'OneDrive - Stark Research Labs', 'ROCBA Dropbox', 'Your team Dropbox', plus a 'Stark Research Labs' folder. The profile also contains an NTUSER.DAT registry hive.
claim.interpretation: The fredr account is provisioned with an unusually broad set of personal and third-party cloud-sync clients (Google Drive, iCloud, two Dropbox roots) alongside the corporate 'OneDrive - Stark Research Labs'. The co-location of corporate and personal cloud-sync roots in one profile gives multiple candidate channels for moving company files off the host; their contents warrant examination. This does not by itself show any file left the host.
